Use Aequor instead , you can try Pontos - Arachne - Caecus - Celeste/Winkle . Its more defensive i think but Pontos and Arachne can help you defeat mobs and boss
I forgot to change it that is why 🙂‍↕️
I had Doresain in there before

- Give Life Drain to Thais for her Covenant 
- Give Thais her Sig WoD, or at the very least Keyflare WoDs in general
- Give Pickman a Dreams of Medicine for his Covenant 
- Raise Pickman to E2 for more comfortable Paintings / Relics
- Raise Sorel to OE, for that sweet sweet Exalt Damage
- Give Sorel an Analysis of Death instead for her SR WoD, or even better an +12 Bloody Feast
- Sorel Covenant can remain as Crimson Pulse, but you could consider replacing it with April Tribute or Chamber 36
Oh I meant specifically like which units I should swap out, but thanks!

These are my current characters. I'm new to the game, so I'd really appreciate any team building advice 🥺
*oh*

assuming that you're just early game: anything works, really
Pontos is *fine* in early game, mostly just because *he can just red tentacle stance then slam once ezpz*, though however
- Easiest most forgiving (rn): Pontos Caecus Faros Sanga (or p much anyone): pretty much a hella safe team in everything, also Pontos will give you Divine Aequor (rn is Benthos Aequor but eh whatever lmao) with 3 turns cooldown after switching tentacle color (like if u switch from blue to red, the next turn you can't use red again and it'll have 3 turns cooldown)
- the other old man team: Helot Salvador Aigis flex, also can Ramona, she's pretty goat: pretty much ez Caro team, Salvador can gib beeg shield and pump the Furnace (the ballz) to heal, can start with that team if you want to learn Caro mechanics (of embryo and furnace)

big note that the game starts to spike harder in ch6 boss and ch7, ch8 as well, so be careful, you have earlier chapters to learn things and read things
also levelling skill levels is good
after clearing ch9, you'll enter arc2, where Soulforge (in Talent tab) is applied. Soulforge is also a big thingy in dtide as well, tho not in Pdive
oh wow tysm! i will try to build that team

oh btw: upcoming reruns are all strong
Ghelot is beeg dps but also need to be careful (really want e3, also e1 or e2 is fine but pretty copium)
Corpo is a discard-focus dps-in-warden trenchcoat with fishing spear, also really want dupe (e2+)
Pollux is the angri birb, also want dupe (e2) for stability overall, altho kinda workable in e0 but *it's a bit copium*

```
Talents
Destined Threads

When Arachne is in the team, the Ultra Realm reweaves into Singularity: Ultra.
Dimension Shuttle: After playing the first Command Card each turn, a Fleeting, Basic Copy of it will be put into Ultra Space and granted 25 stacks of Singularity Beacon. This cannot trigger during Ultra Rounds. If a card triggers Dimension Shuttle, it also benefits from the effects of Singularity Beacon.
Singularity Beacon: For each stack, a card's Final DMG, STR & Tentacle DMG gain, Fixed-value Poison & Counter each +2%; Final Shield, HP Recovery, and STR▼ each +1%. This effect clears when the card is played. Cards with this effect cannot trigger Dimension Shuttle.
Ultra Space: When full, beginning the Ultra Round does not end the turn nor does it start one; discard all non-Retain cards, resolve their Fleeting effects, pull all cards from Ultra Space, and set Arithmetica to 5. This current turn continues as an Ultra Round.
Singularity Annihilation: Pull the leftmost card out from Ultra Space and into your hand. 3 turns cooldown.
At the start of investigation, gain +50% DMG Amplification and 15 stacks of Singularity Prism.
Singularity Prism: All Command Cards' effects increase as though they had this many stacks of Singularity Beacon.
Ultra Mastery: For each 1 point of Realm Mastery, Singularity Beacon stacks from Singularity Prism and Dimension Shuttle increase by 0.05%.
Pure Ultra: If the team consists only of Ultra or Chaos Awakeners, the Ultra Mastery effect and Realm DMG Amplification are doubled.
```

well, everything except damage
Welp

What horla does:
When you leap with horla's cards, you trigger a permanent buff specific to which card you leap with
strike (they all have some other name, but it's basically her strike): gives crit rate and damage amp %
defense: healing, shielding and strength down effects all increased
skill 1 (0 cost iirc): restores aliemus to a chosen unit, leap buff increases all your aliemus gain from most sources
skill 2(2 cost): gives you strength, the opponent strength down, leap buff increases your strength gain from any source

when you ult, you get healed a bit, get some strength, give a tiny bit of aliemus (I think?) and gets 10% crit rate, and one of these effects is activated at 150% effectiveness, depending on which permanent buff is active
oh, it's only one perm buff at a time, so only leap when you wanna change the buff
to avoid her being *utterly stupidly OP*
Damn she really does do everything
yeah
Is there like a permanent buff that's usually recommended to have up or are they all valid
Snarl mostly
they're all good. You can change once per turn (technically twice if you leap again with another effect like tinct 2 cost or wanda ult?)
The dmg buff
snarl, from the strike, is the common one
but if you play her with Ghelot, you might want to keep the strength buff one active near the start, to build some extra strength with Thais

you can leap multiple times in ultra turn
